current_phase: Phase 5 - PASS 4 HeyGen tutorial video workflow

current_state.summary: The repo now contains the existing review-polished training email studio plus a mock-first HeyGen tutorial video workflow tied to approved drafts, imported HeyGen source docs, focused implementation knowledge docs, and browser-validated script approval, render, poll, review, and attach flow.

01_google_ai_for_loan_officers.next_priority: Validate the real Google AI capability surface and then tighten the prompt library from Jeremy's preferred outputs.

02_loan_factory_and_terraplus_operations.next_priority: Replace the remaining caution-level operational notes with validated internal workflow artifacts when they are available.

03_sales_marketing_communication_and_con.next_priority: Load approved real message examples so the sales and transparency docs can move from framework level to example-backed level.

04_weekly_training_production_lab.next_priority: Review one generated Monday issue and one generated Thursday issue with Jeremy and tune the template, seed examples, and memory defaults from actual edit patterns.

05_heygen_tutorial_video_workflow.next_priority: Validate the exact live HeyGen avatar, voice, endpoint, and rights inputs before enabling live render mode.

notes: Uploaded HeyGen source docs were imported into continuity/source_packets/2026-03-23_codex_pass4_heygen/ as traceable raw material.
PASS 4 added a HeyGen tutorial video workflow to the existing shell without rebuilding it.
Browser-backed validation on a production-style server confirmed the approved-draft tutorial-video flow in mock mode.
Browser-backed validation approved a draft, generated a tutorial script, approved the script, requested render, polled to completion, and attached the completed mock video.
Browser-backed validation confirmed the completed video player and provider setup posture for HeyGen mock mode.
Mock-mode posture was confirmed with HeyGen provider secrets absent.
README and env placeholder guidance were rechecked against the current mock-mode and Netlify posture.

needs_factual_validation: Real Loan Factory process details and approval owners
Current Google AI product capabilities and internal tool availability
The meaning and intended usage of HAPPA in the Loan Factory context
MOSO and seven-day SLA definitions and approved workflow meaning
Actual rate lock, extension, rate-alert, and consent policy details
Exact HeyGen API endpoint, payload, avatar_id, voice_id, background options, and credit usage details for live mode
needs_compliance_review: Borrower-facing, lead-facing, and realtor-facing templates or messages before live use
Training or communication content that references loan products, rates, approvals, fees, timelines, or borrower scenarios
Operational guides covering rate locks, extensions, rate alerts, AI-assisted 1003 handling, or escalation workflow guidance
AI governance and NPI guidance before it is treated as company policy
Avatar rights, consent posture, and final tutorial-video disclosure or footer language before live use

next_best_move: Confirm the exact live HeyGen avatar, voice, endpoint, and compliance guardrails, then run one live tutorial-video render after Jeremy approves the mock workflow.
